title: ajuga ovalifolia var. angustifolia
intro: Ajuga ovalifolia var. angustifolia, also known as Bugleweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Bugleweed include reducing anxiety and stress, relieving insomnia and restlessness, and lowering blood pressure. Bugleweed can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat conditions such as hyperthyroidism and menopausal symptoms.
